title: Stage 3: Friend-first bridge path
subtitle: After the primary venture route, Tigerclaw can still show a softer connector-first path when the warmer ask is strategically safer.
summary: This stage is the alternative path replay. Instead of going straight to a venture contact, the founder starts with a trusted connector, uses shared context and platform familiarity to earn the next move, and only then steps into an investor conversation.
callouts: This route is softer, but it is still strategic. The point is not to maximize prestige, it is to maximize warmth and response probability.
Katia matters because FrontierTower already sits near Tigerclaw's story and because she can plausibly open the path into Justin at Decasonic.
The ask changes here: do not ask for an investor intro first. Ask for a steer into the right conversation.

likelyInvestorDestination.name: Justin Patel -> Decasonic
likelyInvestorDestination.description: A softer AI + Web3 investor path reached through trust and founder context instead of a direct fundraise ask.

narrative.warmPathSummary: This route is intentionally softer. Instead of reopening the investor conversation directly, Tigerclaw uses Katia as the trusted bridge into Justin and only then steps into Decasonic's investor context.
narrative.whyThisPerson: Katia is the right connector here because the relationship carries genuine founder overlap and FrontierTower already sits close to Tigerclaw's story. That makes the first message feel like a request for perspective, not a hidden ask for capital.
narrative.whyNow: The friend-first route matters right now because it gives the founder a lower-friction re-entry into the network when a direct investor ask would feel too abrupt. It keeps momentum without spending social trust too early.
narrative.whyThisRankedAboveOthers: It ranks below Harrison on directness, but above the rest of the bridge layer on warmth and response probability. Katia is closer to the founder's lived context and has a more credible handoff into Justin than the other operator routes.
narrative.suggestedAsk: Ask Katia for a steer into the right Decasonic conversation rather than requesting an investor intro directly. Keep it specific and founder-to-founder.
supportingProof: FrontierTower already has context on Tigerclaw, so the bridge is not starting from zero.
Shared founder and community context makes the ask feel earned rather than opportunistic.
Justin at Decasonic becomes reachable through a human bridge instead of a cold investor ask.
narrative.likelyObjection: If the message feels like a disguised investor ask, Katia may protect the relationship by avoiding the handoff.
narrative.nextBestStep: Message Katia first, ask for a quick steer, and only escalate into Justin once the fit and angle are clear.
narrative.draftMessage: Hey Katia, I have been thinking about how Tigerclaw fits into the AI and Web3 founder conversations you already sit close to. Rather than forcing a direct investor ask from the outside, I wanted to start with you because I think you would have the best read on whether there is a real Decasonic angle here. If I send a short context note and traction snapshot, would you be open to steering me toward the right conversation, even if the answer is just how to frame it better first?

executionRows.label: Route mode
executionRows.value: Friend-first bridge through Katia into Justin and Decasonic.

executionRows.label: Draft posture
executionRows.value: Lead with trust and calibration, not with a direct intro request.

nodes.label: Ask Katia for a steer
nodes.subtitle: Lower-friction than a direct investor ask
